# Workflows

```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/lgtm/grade/python/g/DiamondLightSource/python-workflows.svg?logo=lgtm&logoWidth=18
:alt: 'Language grade: Python'
:target: https://lgtm.com/projects/g/DiamondLightSource/python-workflows/context:python
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/lgtm/alerts/g/DiamondLightSource/python-workflows.svg?logo=lgtm&logoWidth=18
:alt: Total alerts
:target: https://lgtm.com/projects/g/DiamondLightSource/python-workflows/alerts/
```

Workflows enables light-weight services to process tasks in a message-oriented
environment.

It is comprised of a communications layer (`workflows.transport`) that provides a
common interface to queues and topics over different transport providers, a
service abstraction layer (`workflows.frontend`) which encapsulates a service in
a separate process, tools for status reporting, logging and controlling
services via a transport mechanism, and a service class skeleton alongside
example services (`workflows.services.common_service` et al.).
To achieve more complex workflows services can be interconnected using
[recipes] whereby the output of one service can be directed
onwards to other services.

[recipes]: https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/python-workflows/tree/main/src/workflows/recipe/README.MD

modules.name: workflows.recipe.validate
documentation:
Runs the json validator on a json file and raises an exception if there is an error
Intended to be used as a hook for repositories which are developing recipes

Example of how this could be used in a .pre-commit-config.yaml file:
```
# Validate json recipes
- repo: https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/python-workflows
  hooks:
  - name: Recipe validation
    id: recipe-validation
    language: python
    files: ^recipes/
    entry: workflows.validate-recipe
```

functions.name: workflows.recipe.validate.validate_recipe
documentation:
Reads a json file, tries to turn it into a recipe and then validates it.
Exits on exception with non-zero error

functions.name: workflows.recipe.validate.main
documentation: Run the program from entry point

functions.name: workflows.recipe._wrap_subscription
documentation:
Internal method to create an intercepting function for incoming messages
to interpret recipes. This function is then used to subscribe to a channel
on the transport layer.

> ```
> param transport_layer
>
> Reference to underlying transport object.
>
> param subscription_call
>
> Reference to the subscribing function of the
> transport layer.
>
> param channel
>
> Channel name to subscribe to.
>
> param callback
>
> Real function to be called when messages are received.
> The callback will pass three arguments,
> a RecipeWrapper object (details below), the header as
> a dictionary structure, and the message.
>
> param allow_non_recipe_messages
>
> Pass on incoming messages that do not
> include recipe information. In this case the first
> argument to the callback function will be 'None'.
>
> param log_extender
>
> If the recipe contains useful contextual information
> for log messages, such as a unique ID which can be used
> to connect all messages originating from the same
> recipe, then the information will be passed to this
> function, which must be a context manager factory.
>
> return
>
> Return value of call to subscription_call.
> ```

functions.name: workflows.recipe.wrap_subscribe
documentation:
Listen to a queue on the transport layer, similar to the subscribe call in
transport/common_transport.py. Intercept all incoming messages and parse
for recipe information.
See common_transport.subscribe for possible additional keyword arguments.

> ```
> param transport_layer
>
> Reference to underlying transport object.
>
> param channel
>
> Queue name to subscribe to.
>
> param callback
>
> Function to be called when messages are received.
> The callback will pass three arguments,
> a RecipeWrapper object (details below), the header as
> a dictionary structure, and the message.
>
> return
>
> A unique subscription ID
> ```

functions.name: workflows.recipe.wrap_subscribe_broadcast
documentation:
Listen to a topic on the transport layer, similar to the
subscribe_broadcast call in transport/common_transport.py. Intercept all
incoming messages and parse for recipe information.
See common_transport.subscribe_broadcast for possible arguments.

> ```
> param transport_layer
>
> Reference to underlying transport object.
>
> param channel
>
> Topic name to subscribe to.
>
> param callback
>
> Function to be called when messages are received.
> The callback will pass three arguments,
> a RecipeWrapper object (details below), the header as
> a dictionary structure, and the message.
>
> return
>
> A unique subscription ID
> ```

Convert a recipe that has been stored as serialized json string to a

methods.name: workflows.recipe.recipe.Recipe._sanitize

Clean up a recipe that may have been stored as serialized json string.
Convert any numerical pointers that are stored as strings to integers.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.recipe.Recipe.pretty

documentation: Testing for presence of recipe elements.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.recipe.Recipe.__eq__
documentation:
Overload equality operator (!=) to allow comparing recipe objects
with one another and with their string representations.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.recipe.Recipe.__ne__
documentation:
Overload inequality operator (!=) to allow comparing recipe objects
with one another and with their string representations.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.recipe.Recipe.__hash__
documentation: Recipe objects are mutable and therefore should not be hashable.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.recipe.Recipe.validate
documentation:
Check whether the encoded recipe is valid. It must describe a directed
acyclical graph, all connections must be defined, etc.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.recipe.Recipe.apply_parameters
workflows.recipe.recipe.Recipe.merge
documentation:
Merge two recipes together, returning a single recipe containing all
nodes.
Note: This does NOT yet return a minimal recipe.
\:param other: A Recipe object that should be merged with the current

> Recipe object.

```
return

A new Recipe object containing information from both recipes.
```

class_variables.name: workflows.recipe.recipe.Recipe.recipe
documentation: The processing recipe is encoded in this dictionary.

Object containing a processing recipe that can be passed to services.
A recipe describes how all involved services are connected together, how
data should be passed and how errors should be handled.

classes.name: workflows.recipe.wrapper.RecipeWrapper

methods.name: workflows.recipe.wrapper.RecipeWrapper.__init__
documentation:
Create a RecipeWrapper object from a wrapped message.
References to the transport layer are required to send directly to
connected downstream processes.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.wrapper.RecipeWrapper.send
documentation:
Send messages to another service that is connected to the currently
running service via the recipe. The 'send' method will either use a
default channel name, set via the set_default_channel method, or an
unnamed output definition.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.wrapper.RecipeWrapper.send_to
documentation:
Send messages to another service that is connected to the currently
running service via the recipe. Discard messages if the recipe does
not have anything connected to the specified output channel.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.wrapper.RecipeWrapper.set_default_channel
documentation:
Define one named output channel to be equivalent to unnamed output
channels. For this channel send() and send_to() will be identical.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.wrapper.RecipeWrapper.start
documentation:
Trigger the start of a recipe, sending the defined payloads to the
recipients set in the recipe. Any parameters to this function are
passed to the transport send/broadcast methods.
If the wrapped recipe has already been started then a ValueError will
be raised.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.wrapper.RecipeWrapper.checkpoint
documentation:
Send a message to the current recipe destination. This can be used to
keep a state for longer processing tasks.
\:param delay: Delay transport of message by this many seconds

methods.name: workflows.recipe.wrapper.RecipeWrapper.apply_parameters
documentation:
Recursively apply parameter replacement (see recipe.py) to the wrapped
recipe, updating internal references afterwards.
While this operation is useful for testing it should not be used in
production. Replacing parameters means that the recipe changes as it is
passed down the chain of services. This makes debugging very difficult.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.wrapper.RecipeWrapper._generate_full_recipe_message
documentation:
Factory function to generate independent message objects for
downstream recipients with different destinations.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.wrapper.RecipeWrapper._send_to_destinations
documentation:
Send messages to a list of numbered destinations. This is an internal
helper method used by the public 'send' methods.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.wrapper.RecipeWrapper._send_to_destination
documentation: Helper function to send a message to a specific recipe destination.

methods.name: workflows.recipe.wrapper.RecipeWrapper._retry_transport
documentation:
Attempt to send a message, and in case the connection has been lost,
attempt to reconnect. Reconnecting only works on the assumption that
the previous connection did not include any subscriptions, which should
be true for wrappers.

A wrapper object which contains a recipe and a number of functions to make
life easier for recipe users.

classes.name: workflows.contrib.start_service.ServiceStarter

methods.name: workflows.contrib.start_service.ServiceStarter.on_parser_preparation
documentation:
Plugin hook to manipulate the OptionParser object before command line
parsing. If a value is returned here it will replace the OptionParser
object.

methods.name: workflows.contrib.start_service.ServiceStarter.on_parsing
documentation:
Plugin hook to manipulate the command line parsing results.
A tuple of values can be returned, which will replace (options, args).

methods.name: workflows.contrib.start_service.ServiceStarter.on_transport_factory_preparation
documentation:
Plugin hook to intercept/manipulate newly created Transport factories
before first invocation.

methods.name: workflows.contrib.start_service.ServiceStarter.on_transport_preparation
documentation:
Plugin hook to intercept/manipulate newly created Transport objects
before connecting.

methods.name: workflows.contrib.start_service.ServiceStarter.before_frontend_construction
documentation:
Plugin hook to manipulate the Frontend object constructor arguments. If
a value is returned here it will replace the keyword arguments
dictionary passed to the constructor.

methods.name: workflows.contrib.start_service.ServiceStarter.on_frontend_preparation
documentation:
Plugin hook to manipulate the Frontend object before starting it. If a
value is returned here it will replace the Frontend object.

methods.name: workflows.contrib.start_service.ServiceStarter.run
documentation:
Example command line interface to start services.
\:param cmdline_args: List of command line arguments to pass to parser
\:param program_name: Name of the command line tool to display in help
\:param version: Version number to print when run with '--version'

A helper class to start a workflows service from the command line.
A number of hooks are provided so that this class can be subclassed and
used in a number of scenarios.

functions.name: workflows.util.generate_unique_host_id
documentation:
Generate a unique ID, that is somewhat guaranteed to be unique among all
instances running at the same time.

functions.name: workflows.logging.get_exception_source
documentation:
Returns full file path, file name, line number, function name, and line contents
causing the last exception.

classes.name: workflows.logging.CallbackHandler

methods.name: workflows.logging.CallbackHandler.__init__
documentation: Set up a handler instance, record the callback function.

methods.name: workflows.logging.CallbackHandler.prepare
documentation:
Prepares a record for queuing. The object returned by this method is
enqueued.
The base implementation formats the record to merge the message
and arguments, and removes unpickleable items from the record
in-place.
You might want to override this method if you want to convert
the record to a dict or JSON string, or send a modified copy
of the record while leaving the original intact.

methods.name: workflows.logging.CallbackHandler.emit
documentation:
Send a LogRecord to the callback function, after preparing it
for serialization.
This handler sends logrecords to a callback function.

Reports will always cover the most recent period of summation_period

methods.name: workflows.frontend.utilization.UtilizationStatistics.report
documentation:
Return a dictionary of different status codes and the percentage of time
spent in each throughout the last summation_period seconds.
Truncate the aggregated history appropriately.

Generate statistics about the percentage of time spent in different
statuses over a fixed time slice. This class is not thread-safe.

A sample implementation of a status monitor showing all running services.
To use this example class, pass in a transport object and call the run()
method.

functions.name: workflows.services.lookup
documentation:
Find a service class based on a name.
\:param service: Name of the service
\:return: A service class

functions.name: workflows.services.get_known_services
documentation:
Return a dictionary of all known services.
\:return: A dictionary containing entries { service name : service class factory }

> A factory is a function that takes no arguments and returns an
> uninstantiated service class.

classes.name: workflows.services.sample_producer.SampleProducer

methods.name: workflows.services.sample_producer.SampleProducer.initializing
documentation:
Service initialization. This function is run before any commands are
received from the frontend. This is the place to request channel
subscriptions with the messaging layer, and register callbacks.
This function can be overridden by specific service implementations.

methods.name: workflows.services.sample_producer.SampleProducer.create_message
documentation: Create and send a unique message for this service.

An example service building on top of the workflow.services architecture,
demonstrating how this architecture can be used.
This service generates messages into a queue.

classes.name: workflows.services.sample_pipethrough.SamplePipethrough

documentation: Consume message and send to output pipe.

An example services building on top of the workflow.services architecture,
demonstrating how this architecture can be used.
This services consumes messages off a queue, and forwards to another.

classes.name: workflows.services.sample_transaction.SampleTxn

methods.name: workflows.services.sample_transaction.SampleTxn.initializing
documentation: Subscribe to a channel. Received messages must be acknowledged.

methods.name: workflows.services.sample_transaction.SampleTxn.crashpoint
documentation: Return true if the service should malfunction at this point.

methods.name: workflows.services.sample_transaction.SampleTxn.receive_message
documentation: Receive a message

An example service building on top of the workflow.services architecture,
demonstrating how this architecture can be used.
This service consumes a messages off one queue and places it into another.
Transactions are used to guarantee correct message handling.
The service is deliberately unreliable and prone to failure.

classes.name: workflows.services.sample_transaction.SampleTxnProducer

methods.name: workflows.services.sample_transaction.SampleTxnProducer.initializing
documentation:
Service initialization. This function is run before any commands are
received from the frontend. This is the place to request channel
subscriptions with the messaging layer, and register callbacks.
This function can be overridden by specific service implementations.

methods.name: workflows.services.sample_transaction.SampleTxnProducer.create_message
documentation: Create and send a unique message for this service.

An example service building on top of the workflow.services architecture,
demonstrating how this architecture can be used.
This service generates messages for the Transaction example.

classes.name: workflows.services.common_service.Status

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.Status.__init__
documentation:
Each status is defined as a tuple of a unique integer value and a
descriptive string. These are available via enum properties

class_variables.name: workflows.services.common_service.Status.NONE
documentation: Node has no service instance loaded

class_variables.name: workflows.services.common_service.Status.TEARDOWN
documentation: Node is shutting down

Internal service status codes
---------------------------------------------
These codes will be sent to the frontend to indicate the current state of
the main loop regardless of the status text, which can be set freely by
the specific service.

classes.name: workflows.services.common_service.Priority
documentation:
Priorities for the service-internal priority queue. This ensures that eg.
frontend commands are always processed before timer events.

classes.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.initializing
documentation:
Service initialization. This function is run before any commands are
received from the frontend. This is the place to request channel
subscriptions with the messaging layer, and register callbacks.
This function can be overridden by specific service implementations.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.in_shutdown
documentation:
Service shutdown. This function is run before the service is terminated.
No more commands are received, but communications can still be sent.
This function can be overridden by specific service implementations.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.__init__
documentation:
Service constructor. Parameters include optional references to two
pipes: frontend= for messages from the service to the frontend,
and commands= for messages from the frontend to the service.
A dictionary can optionally be passed with environment=, which is then
available to the service during runtime.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.__send_to_frontend
documentation: Put a message in the pipe for the frontend.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.start_transport
documentation: If a transport object has been defined then connect it now.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.stop_transport
documentation: If a transport object has been defined then tear it down.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService._transport_interceptor
documentation:
Takes a callback function and returns a function that takes headers and
messages and places them on the main service queue.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.connect
documentation:
Inject pipes connecting the service to the frontend. Two arguments are
supported: frontend= for messages from the service to the frontend,
and commands= for messages from the frontend to the service.
The injection should happen before the service is started, otherwise the
underlying file descriptor references may not be handled correctly.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.extend_log
documentation:
A context wherein a specified extra field in log messages is populated
with a fixed value. This affects all log messages within the context.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.__command_queue_listener
documentation:
Function to continuously retrieve data from the frontend. Commands are
sent to the central priority queue. If the pipe from the frontend is
closed the service shutdown is initiated. Check every second if service
has shut down, then terminate.
This function is run by a separate daemon thread, which is started by
the __start_command_queue_listener function.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.__start_command_queue_listener
documentation:
Start the function __command_queue_listener in a separate thread. This
function continuously listens to the pipe connected to the frontend.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService._log_send
documentation: Forward log records to the frontend.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService._register
documentation: Register a callback function for a specific message band.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService._register_idle
documentation:
Register a callback function that is run when idling for a given
time span (in seconds).

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.__update_service_status
documentation: Set the internal status of the service object, and notify frontend.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.__send_service_status_to_frontend
documentation: Actually send the internal status of the service object to the frontend.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.get_name
documentation: Get the name for this service.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService._set_name
documentation: Set a new name for this service, and notify the frontend accordingly.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService._request_termination
documentation: Terminate the service from the frontend side

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService._shutdown
documentation: Terminate the service from the service side.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.initialize_logging
documentation:
Reset the logging for the service process. All logged messages are
forwarded to the frontend. If any filtering is desired, then this must
take place on the service side.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.start
documentation:
Start listening to command queue, process commands in main loop,
set status, etc...
This function is most likely called by the frontend in a separate
process.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.process_uncaught_exception
documentation:
This is called to handle otherwise uncaught exceptions from the service.
The service will terminate either way, but here we can do things such as
gathering useful environment information and logging for posterity.

methods.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.__process_command
documentation: Process an incoming command message from the frontend.

class_variables.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService._logger_name
documentation: The logger can be accessed via self.log

class_variables.name: workflows.services.common_service.CommonService.human_readable_state
documentation: lgtm

Base class for workflow services. A service is a piece of software that runs
in an isolated environment, communicating only via pipes with the outside
world. Units of work are injected via a pipe. Results, status and log
messages, etc. are written out via a pipe. Any task can be encapsulated
as a service, for example a service that counts spots on an image passed
as a filename, and returns the number of counts.

To instantiate a service two Pipe-like objects should be passed to the
constructors, one to communicate from the service to the frontend, one to
communicate from the frontend to the service.

classes.name: workflows.services.common_service.Commands
documentation: A list of command strings used for communicating with the frontend.

classes.name: workflows.frontend.Frontend

methods.name: workflows.frontend.Frontend.__init__
documentation:
Create a frontend instance. Connect to the transport layer, start any
requested service, begin broadcasting status information and listen
for control commands.
\:param restart_service:

> If the service process dies unexpectedly the frontend should start
> a new instance.

```
param service

A class or name of the class to be instantiated in a subprocess as
service.

param transport

Either the name of a transport class, a transport class, or a
transport class object.

param transport_command_channel

An optional channel of a transport subscription to be listened to for
commands.

param verbose_service

If set, run services with increased logging level (DEBUG).

param environment

An optional dictionary that is passed to started services.
```

methods.name: workflows.frontend.Frontend.update_status
documentation:
Update the service status kept inside the frontend (\_service_status).
The status is broadcast over the network immediately. If the status
changes to IDLE then this message is delayed. The IDLE status is only
broadcast if it is held for over 0.5 seconds.
When the status does not change it is still broadcast every
\_status_interval seconds.
\:param status_code: Either an integer describing the service status

> (see workflows.services.common_service), or None
> if the status is unchanged.

methods.name: workflows.frontend.Frontend.run
documentation:
The main loop of the frontend.
This is where the frontend process will spend most of its time.

methods.name: workflows.frontend.Frontend._iterate_main_loop
documentation:
Collection of steps that are run over and over again in the main loop
of the frontend. Here incoming messages from the service are processed
and forwarded to their corresponding callback methods.

methods.name: workflows.frontend.Frontend.send_command
documentation: Send command to service via the command queue.

methods.name: workflows.frontend.Frontend.process_transport_command
documentation: Parse a command coming in through the transport command subscription

methods.name: workflows.frontend.Frontend.parse_band_log
documentation: Process incoming logging messages from the service.

methods.name: workflows.frontend.Frontend.parse_band_request_termination
documentation: Service declares it should be terminated.

methods.name: workflows.frontend.Frontend.parse_band_set_name
documentation: Process incoming message indicating service name change.

Returns a dictionary containing all relevant status information to be

methods.name: workflows.frontend.Frontend.exponential_backoff
documentation:
A function that keeps waiting longer and longer the more rapidly it is called.
It can be used to increasingly slow down service starts when they keep failing.

methods.name: workflows.frontend.Frontend.switch_service
documentation:
Start a new service in a subprocess.
\:param new_service: Either a service name or a service class. If not set,

> start up a new instance of the previous class

```
return

True on success, False on failure.
```

methods.name: workflows.frontend.Frontend._terminate_service
documentation:
Force termination of running service.
Disconnect queues, end queue feeder threads.
Wait for service process to clear, drop all references.

The frontend class encapsulates the actual service. It controls the
service process and keeps the connection to the transport layer. It
can process control messages directly, or pass messages on to the
service.

methods.name: workflows.transport.stomp_transport.StompTransport._subscribe
documentation:
Listen to a queue, notify via callback function.
\:param sub_id: ID for this subscription in the transport layer
\:param channel: Queue name to subscribe to
\:param callback: Function to be called when messages are received
\:param kwargs: Further parameters for the transport layer. For example

> acknowledgement: If true receipt of each message needs to be
>
> : acknowledged.
>
> exclusive: Attempt to become exclusive subscriber to the queue.
> ignore_namespace: Do not apply namespace to the destination name
> priority: Consumer priority, messages are sent to higher
>
> > priority consumers whenever possible.
>
> selector: Only receive messages filtered by a selector. See
>
> : <https://activemq.apache.org/activemq-message-properties.html>
>   for potential filter criteria. Uses SQL 92 syntax.

methods.name: workflows.transport.stomp_transport.StompTransport._subscribe_broadcast
documentation:
Listen to a broadcast topic, notify via callback function.
\:param sub_id: ID for this subscription in the transport layer
\:param channel: Topic name to subscribe to
\:param callback: Function to be called when messages are received
\:param kwargs: Further parameters for the transport layer. For example

> ignore_namespace: Do not apply namespace to the destination name
> retroactive: Ask broker to send old messages if possible

methods.name: workflows.transport.stomp_transport.StompTransport._subscribe_temporary
documentation:
Create and then listen to a temporary queue, notify via callback function.
\:param sub_id: ID for this subscription in the transport layer
\:param channel_hint: Name suggestion for the temporary queue
\:param callback: Function to be called when messages are received
\:param kwargs: Further parameters for the transport layer.

> See \_subscribe() above.

```
returns

The name of the temporary queue
```

methods.name: workflows.transport.stomp_transport.StompTransport._send
documentation:
Send a message to a queue.
\:param destination: Queue name to send to
\:param message: A string to be sent
\:param kwargs: Further parameters for the transport layer. For example

> delay: Delay transport of message by this many seconds
> expiration: Optional expiration time, relative to sending time
> headers: Optional dictionary of header entries
> ignore_namespace: Do not apply namespace to the destination name
> persistent: Whether to mark messages as persistent, to be kept
>
> > between broker restarts. Default is 'true'.
>
> transaction: Transaction ID if message should be part of a
>
> : transaction

> headers: Optional dictionary of header entries
> ignore_namespace: Do not apply namespace to the destination name
> transaction: Transaction ID if message should be part of a
>
> > transaction

>

methods.name: workflows.transport.stomp_transport.StompTransport._mangle_for_sending
documentation:
Function that any message will pass through before it being forwarded to
the actual _send* functions.
Stomp only deals with serialized strings, so serialize message as json.

methods.name: workflows.transport.stomp_transport.StompTransport._mangle_for_receiving
documentation:
Function that any message will pass through before it being forwarded to
the receiving subscribed callback functions.
This transport class only deals with serialized strings, so decode
message from json. However anything can come into here, so catch any
deserialization errors.

Abstraction layer for messaging infrastructure. Here we are using ActiveMQ
with STOMP.

functions.name: workflows.transport.common_transport.json_serializer
documentation:
A helper function for JSON serialization, where it can be used as
the default= argument. This function helps the serializer to translate
objects that otherwise would not be understood. Note that this is
one-way only - these objects are not restored on the receiving end.

Drop reference to the callback function after unsubscribing.
Any future messages arriving for that subscription will result in
exceptions being raised.

methods.name: workflows.transport.common_transport.CommonTransport.subscription_callback
documentation:
Retrieve the callback function for a subscription. Raise a
workflows.Error if the subscription does not exist.
All transport callbacks can be intercepted by setting an
interceptor function with subscription_callback_intercept().
\:param subscription: Subscription ID to look up
\:return: Callback function

methods.name: workflows.transport.common_transport.CommonTransport.subscription_callback_set_intercept
documentation:
Set a function to intercept all callbacks. This is useful to, for
example, keep a thread barrier between the transport related functions
and processing functions.
\:param interceptor: A function that takes the original callback function

> and returns a modified callback function. Or None to
> disable interception.

Function that any message will pass through before it being forwarded to
the actual _send* functions.
-- Internal message mangling functions -----------------------------------
Some transport mechanisms will not be able to work with arbitrary objects,
so these functions are used to prepare a message for sending/receiving.
The canonical example is serialization/deserialization, see stomp_transport

the receiving subscribed callback functions.

subscriptions and transactions.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport.PikaTransport._call_message_callback
documentation: Rewrite and redirect a pika callback to the subscription function

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport.PikaTransport._subscribe
documentation:
Listen to a queue, notify via callback function.

Waits until subscription is complete.

Args:
    sub_id: Internal ID to keep track of this subscription.
    channel: Queue name to subscribe to
    callback: Function to be called when messages are received
    acknowledgement:
        Each message will need to be explicitly acknowledged.
        Not compatible with reconnectable.
    reconnectable:
        Allow automatic re-establishing of the subscription over
        a new connection, in case of connection failure. This
        cannot be used if acknowledgement is also set.
    prefetch_count:
        How many messages will be prefetched for the subscription.
        This makes little difference unless acknowledgement is
        also set.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport.PikaTransport._subscribe_broadcast
documentation:
Listen to a FANOUT exchange, notify via callback function.

Waits until subscription is complete.

Args:
    sub_id: Internal ID for this subscription
    channel: Name of the exchange to bind to
    callback: Function to be called when messages are received
    reconnectable:
        Can we reconnect to this exchange if the connection is
        lost. Currently, this means that messages can be missed
        if they are sent while we are reconnecting.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport.PikaTransport._subscribe_temporary
documentation:
Create and then listen to a temporary queue, notify via callback function.

Wait until subscription is complete.

Args:
    sub_id: Internal ID for this subscription
    channel_hint: Name suggestion for the temporary queue
    callback: Function to be called when messages are received
    acknowledgement:
        Each message will need to be explicitly acknowledged.
Returns:
    The name of the temporary queue

documentation:

\:param sub_id: Consumer Tag to cancel

    headers: Further arbitrary headers to pass to pika
    delay: Delay transport of message by this many seconds
    expiration: Optional TTL expiration time, in seconds, relative to sending time
    transaction: Transaction ID if message should be part of a transaction
    kwargs: Arbitrary arguments for other transports. Ignored.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport.PikaTransport._mangle_for_sending
documentation:
Function that any message will pass through before it being forwarded to
the actual _send* functions.
Pika only deals with serialized strings, so serialize message as json.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport.PikaTransport._mangle_for_receiving
documentation:
Function that any message will pass through before it being forwarded to
the receiving subscribed callback functions.
This transport class only deals with serialized strings, so decode
message from json. However anything can come into here, so catch any
deserialization errors.

Abstraction layer for messaging infrastructure for RabbitMQ via pika.

Pika methods with both the BlockingConnection and SelectConnection adapters
take control over the thread, and temporarily release it via callbacks.
So to make pika work with the workflows model we need a thread that only
runs pika.

class_variables.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThreadStatus.NEW
documentation: The thread has not started connecting yet

classes.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaSubscriptionKind
documentation:
Represents a kind of subscription, for reconnection

Attributes:
    DIRECT: A subscription to a named exchange and queue.
    FANOUT: A subscription to a fanout exchange. An exclusive queue is created.

classes.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaSubscription
documentation:
Persistently represent a subscription

Attributes:
    arguments: Any extra arguments to pass through to pika
    auto_ack: Should this subscription auto-acknowledge messages?
    destination:
        The target for subscription. This is the exchange name if
        subscribing to broadcasts with an ephemeral queue, or the
        queue name if subscribing to a queue directly.
    kind: What type of subscription this is.
    on_message_callback: The function called by Pika on messages
    prefetch_count: How many messages are we allowed to prefetch
    queue:
        The queue this subscription is actually physically bound to
    reconnectable: Are we allowed to reconnect to this subscription

classes.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread.state
documentation: Read the current connection state

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread.stop
documentation:
Request termination, including disconnection and cleanup if necessary.

This will not block. Please call join() to wait until the thread
has terminated.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread.join
documentation:
Wait until the thread terminates.

Args:
    timeout:
        When not None, a floating point number specifying a
        timeout for the operation in seconds (or fractions thereof).
    re_raise:
        If the thread terminated due to an exception, then raise
        this exception in the callers thread. Equivalent to calling
        'raise_if_exception' after 'join()'.
    stop:
        Call .stop() to request termination.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread.wait_for_connection
documentation:
Safely wait until the thread has connected and is communicating with the server.

When the timeout argument is present and not None, it should be
a floating point number specifying a timeout for the operation
in seconds (or fractions thereof).

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread.raise_if_exception
documentation: If the thread has failed with an exception, raise it in the callers thread.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread.subscribe_queue
documentation:
Subscribe to a queue. Thread-safe.

Args:
    queue: The queue to listen for messages on
    callback: The function to call when receiving messages on this queue
    subscription_id: Internal ID representing this subscription.
    auto_ack: Should this subscription auto-acknowledge messages?
    prefetch_count: How many messages are we allowed to prefetch
    reconnectable: Are we allowed to reconnect to this subscription

Returns:
    A Future representing the subscription state. It will be set
    to an empty value upon subscription success.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread.subscribe_broadcast
documentation:
Subscribe to a broadcast exchange. Thread-safe.

Args:
    exchange: The queue to listen for messages on
    callback: The function to call when receiving messages on this queue
    auto_ack: Should this subscription auto-acknowledge messages?
    subscription_id: Internal ID representing this subscription.
    prefetch_count: How many messages are we allowed to prefetch
    reconnectable:
        Are we allowed to reconnect to this subscription?
        **Warning**: Reconnecting to broadcast exchanges has the
            potential for dropping messages sent while disconnected.

Returns:
    A Future representing the subscription state. It will be set
    to an empty value upon subscription success.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread.subscribe_temporary
documentation:
Create and then subscribe to a temporary queue. Thread-safe.

Args:
    queue: The queue to listen for messages on, may be empty
    callback: The function to call when receiving messages on this queue
    subscription_id: Internal ID representing this subscription.
    auto_ack: Should this subscription auto-acknowledge messages?

Returns:
    A Future representing the resulting queue name.
    It will be set upon subscription success.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread.send
documentation: Send a message. Thread-safe.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread.tx_select
documentation:
Set a channel to transaction mode. Thread-safe.
\:param transaction_id: ID for this transaction in the transport layer.
\:param subscription_id: Tie the transaction to a specific channel containing this subscription.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread.tx_rollback
documentation:
Abort a transaction and roll back all operations. Thread-safe.
\:param transaction_id: ID of transaction to be aborted.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread.tx_commit
documentation:
Commit a transaction.
\:param transaction_id: ID of transaction to be committed. Thread-safe..

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread.connection_alive
documentation:
Is the connection object connected, or in the process of reconnecting?

This will return True if the object is connected right now, or there is
a potential that this object will be reconnected in the future, even if
the connection is lost at this point.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread._debug_close_connection
documentation: NOTE: With reconnection lifecycle this probably doesn't make sense
on its own. It might make sense to add this returning a
connection-specific 'token' - presumably the user might want
to ensure that a connection is still the same connection
and thus adhering to various within-connection guarantees.
Remove this until we understand where it might be necessary.
@property
def connected(self):
Has a connection been successfully started at least once, and isn't in the process of shutting down.
Note that this might have changed by the time that you make a
decision based on this property.
return self.connection_alive and self._connected.is_set()
PikaThread Internal methods

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread._get_shared_channel
documentation: Get the shared (no prefetch) channel. Create if necessary.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread._recreate_subscriptions
documentation: Resubscribe all existing subscriptions

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread._register_future_and_callback
documentation:
Queue a callback with the connection, and record its future.

Recording the future allows us to keep track of them even
following an unexpected connection loss, and either send them
upon reconnection or mark them as failed.

methods.name: workflows.transport.pika_transport._PikaThread._add_subscription_in_thread
documentation:
Add a subscription to the pika connection.

Will be called on the connection thread.

Asynchronously handle a pika connection

This will automatically reconnect (if appropriate), and manage
persistent subscriptions across reconnections.

Args:
    connection_parameters:
        How to connect to the AMPQ server. This is an iterable of
        possible connections, which will be attempted in a random
        order.
    reconnection_attempts:
        How many times to consecutively attempt connection before
        giving up. Connecting successfully (and reattaching any
        reconnectable queues) resets this counter. Successive
        attempts will wait longer before reattempting.
